# DevTools by developers.do - Complete Documentation

> A collection of free developer tools that run 100% client-side in your browser. No backend. All tool processing happens locally on the user's device. Anonymized analytics are used to improve the site.

## About DevTools

DevTools is a suite of essential utilities for software developers built with privacy as the core principle. Key characteristics:

- **100% Client-Side**: All tools run entirely in the browser using JavaScript, WebAssembly, and Web APIs
- **No Data Collection**: User input is never transmitted to any server
- **Anonymized Analytics**: Google Analytics is used with consent controls. No tracking cookies without user consent.
- **Works Offline**: After initial page load, most tools work without internet
- **Open Source**: Built with Next.js, React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS

Website: https://developers.do

---

## Security Tools

### SHA-256/512 Hash Generator
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/sha256-generator

Generate cryptographically secure hashes using SHA-256 (64 characters) or SHA-512 (128 characters). Uses the Web Crypto API for reliable, fast hashing. Ideal for:
- File integrity verification
- Password hashing (with proper salting)
- Digital signatures
- Data deduplication

### SHA-1 Hash Generator
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/sha1-generator

Generate SHA-1 hashes (40 characters). Note: SHA-1 is no longer considered cryptographically secure. Use only for:
- Checksums
- Cache busting
- Non-security-critical deduplication
- Legacy system compatibility

### MD5 Hash Generator
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/md5-generator

Generate MD5 hashes (32 characters). Note: MD5 is cryptographically broken. Use only for:
- Checksums
- Cache keys
- Non-security purposes

### JWT Decoder
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/jwt-decoder

Decode and inspect JSON Web Tokens without sending them to a server. Displays:
- Header (algorithm, type)
- Payload (claims, expiration, issuer)
- Signature (for inspection, not verification)

Useful for debugging authentication flows and understanding token contents.

### Secrets Scanner
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/secrets-scanner

Scan text, logs, configuration files, or code for accidentally leaked secrets including:
- AWS access keys and secret keys
- GitHub tokens
- Stripe API keys
- Google API keys
- Private keys (RSA, SSH)
- Database connection strings
- Generic API keys and tokens

### SAML Decoder
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/saml-decoder

Decode Base64-encoded SAML Requests and Responses, with auto-detection of deflate compression. Extracts key fields: Issuer, NameID, Conditions, Attributes, and more. Useful for:
- Debugging SSO login flows
- Inspecting what an IdP or SP is sending
- Troubleshooting SAML authentication errors

### SAML Metadata Parser
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/saml-metadata-parser

Parse SAML Metadata XML from any IdP or SP (Okta, Azure AD, ADFS, etc.) and extract Entity ID, SSO/ACS endpoints, X.509 certificates, NameID formats, and organization info. Useful for:
- Setting up SSO integrations
- Verifying metadata before deployment
- Extracting certificate details from federation metadata

### SAML Certificate Inspector
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/saml-cert-inspector

Inspect X.509 certificates from SAML metadata, SAML responses, or raw PEM/DER. View subject, issuer, validity period, fingerprints (SHA-1, SHA-256), key usage, and SANs. Useful for:
- Checking SAML signing certificate expiry
- Validating certificate identity before configuring SSO
- Comparing certificates between environments

### SAML Assertion Builder
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/saml-builder

Generate test SAML Responses, AuthnRequests, and LogoutRequests from form fields. Output as raw XML, Base64, or Base64+Deflate. Useful for:
- Creating test fixtures for SSO integration tests
- Simulating IdP responses in development
- Mocking SAML authentication flows

### CSR Decoder
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/csr-decoder

Decode PKCS#10 Certificate Signing Requests (CSRs) and inspect all fields: subject (CN, O, OU, C), SANs, requested extensions, signature algorithm, public key details, and fingerprints. Accepts PEM format. Useful for:
- Verifying CSR contents before submitting to a CA
- Checking SANs and subject fields
- Auditing certificate requests

### Clawdbot Security Scanner
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/clawdbot-security-scanner

Audit Clawdbot, Moltbot, and OpenClaw AI agent configurations for security vulnerabilities. Detects exposed gateway APIs, unsafe shell access, credential leaks in config, prompt injection risks, and overly permissive MCP settings. Useful for:
- Security review of AI agent deployments
- Pre-production configuration audits
- Identifying misconfigured permissions

### Password Generator
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/password-generator

Generate cryptographically secure random passwords using `crypto.getRandomValues()`. Options include:
- Password length
- Uppercase/lowercase letters
- Numbers
- Special characters
- Multiple passwords at once

---

## Formatting Tools

### JSON Prettifier
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/json-prettifier

Format, validate, and beautify JSON data. Features:
- Syntax highlighting
- Error detection with line numbers
- Minify option
- Copy formatted output

### XML Prettifier
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/xml-prettifier

Format and validate XML data with proper indentation and syntax highlighting.

### HL7 Parser
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/hl7-parser

Read and parse HL7 v2 healthcare messages into structured JSON. Features:
- Segment and field breakdown
- Repetition/component/subcomponent parsing
- Message metadata extraction (type, trigger, version, control ID)
- Client-side processing (no server transmission)

### HL7 to FHIR Converter
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/hl7-to-fhir

Convert HL7 v2 messages (ADT, ORU, ORM, VXU, SIU) to FHIR R4 Bundles. Maps segments to Patient, Encounter, Observation, and other FHIR resources. Useful for:
- Healthcare interoperability projects
- FHIR migration from legacy HL7 v2 systems
- Testing FHIR pipelines with real HL7 data

### Diff / Text Compare
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/diff-compare

Compare two texts side by side with color-coded line-by-line diffs. Highlights added, removed, and unchanged lines. Useful for:
- Comparing config file versions
- Reviewing text changes before committing
- Spotting differences in API responses or log outputs

### Stack Trace Analyzer
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/stacktrace-analyzer

Analyze stack traces to identify root cause frames, explain error types, and suggest fixes. Separates application code from framework internals. Supports JavaScript, Python, Java, C#, Go, PHP, and Ruby. Useful for:
- Understanding unfamiliar exceptions quickly
- Identifying the exact line causing an error
- Debugging production errors from logs

### String Similarity
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/string-similarity

Compare two strings using multiple algorithms: Jaro-Winkler, Levenshtein distance, Dice coefficient, and Hamming distance. Useful for:
- Fuzzy string matching
- Detecting near-duplicate records
- Evaluating string comparison algorithm behavior

### JSON to JSDoc
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/json-to-jsdoc

Convert JSON objects to JSDoc @typedef comments for JavaScript type hints without TypeScript. Supports optional field detection and nested objects. Useful for:
- Adding type hints to plain JavaScript projects
- Documenting API response shapes in JSDoc

### Base64 Encoder/Decoder
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/base64-encoder

Encode text to Base64 or decode Base64 strings. Useful for:
- Data URLs
- Basic authentication headers
- Email attachments (MIME)
- Embedding binary data in JSON/XML

### Stacktrace Formatter
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/stacktrace-formatter

Format and beautify stack traces from multiple programming languages for easier debugging.

### Beautify Stack Trace
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/beautify-stack-trace

Clean up and beautify messy error stack traces for readability. Supports JavaScript, Python, Java, C#, Go, PHP, and Ruby. Features syntax highlighting, auto-detection, and sensitive data removal. Useful for:
- Making messy error logs readable
- Cleaning up stack traces from production logs
- Sharing formatted traces with teammates

### File Size Converter
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/file-size-converter

Convert file sizes between decimal (SI: KB, MB, GB) and binary (IEC: KiB, MiB, GiB) units.

### Unix Timestamp Converter
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/unix-timestamp

Convert between Unix epoch timestamps and human-readable dates with timezone support. Useful for:
- Debugging timestamps in logs and APIs
- Converting between epoch seconds/milliseconds and dates
- Comparing timestamps across timezones

### Cron Expression Tester
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/cron-tester

Test and parse cron expressions, see them explained in plain English, and preview upcoming run times in any timezone. Supports standard 5-field syntax (minute, hour, day-of-month, month, day-of-week), ranges, lists, steps, month/weekday names, and macros (@hourly, @daily, @weekly, @monthly, @yearly, @midnight). Useful for:
- Validating cron expressions before deploying
- Understanding unfamiliar cron schedules in CI/CD configs
- Verifying when a job will fire across timezones
- Quickly checking common patterns

### Cron Expression Generator
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/cron-generator

Build cron expressions visually with per-field controls (mode tabs, value chips, range inputs) — no need to memorize cron syntax. Pick how each field behaves (every value, every N, specific values, or a range) and the cron string is generated as you click. Live plain-English description and next-run preview included. Useful for:
- Creating cron schedules without learning syntax
- Producing expressions for CI/CD, schedulers, or infrastructure-as-code
- Sharing schedules with non-technical teammates by working from intent rather than text

### URL Encoder / Decoder
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/url-encoder

Encode and decode URLs using encodeURIComponent and encodeURI. Useful for:
- Encoding query parameters
- Debugging URL encoding issues
- Preparing strings for URL inclusion

### YAML ↔ JSON Converter
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/yaml-json-converter

Convert between YAML and JSON with auto-detection of input format. Useful for:
- Converting Kubernetes configs between formats
- Transforming CI/CD pipeline configs
- Working with APIs that accept JSON from YAML sources

### SQL Formatter
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/sql-formatter

Format and prettify SQL queries with support for MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, and more. Useful for:
- Making complex queries readable
- Standardizing SQL formatting in teams
- Preparing queries for code reviews

### Regex Tester
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/regex-tester

Test regular expressions with real-time match highlighting, capture groups, and string replacement. Useful for:
- Building and debugging regex patterns
- Testing patterns against sample text
- Understanding capture group behavior

### Regex Library
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/regex-library

Browse and copy common regular expressions for validation, extraction, and text processing. Includes patterns for emails, URLs, IPs, dates, and more.

### Number Base Converter
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/number-base-converter

Convert numbers between binary, octal, decimal, and hexadecimal with two's complement support. Useful for:
- Low-level programming and debugging
- Understanding binary representations
- Working with hex color codes or memory addresses

---

## Code Generators & Converters

### JSON to TypeScript
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/json-to-typescript

Convert JSON objects to TypeScript interfaces. Automatically infers types and generates properly typed interfaces.

### JSON to C#
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/json-to-csharp

Convert JSON to C# classes with proper property types and attributes.

### JSON to Swift
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/json-to-swift

Convert JSON to Swift structs with Codable conformance.

### JSON to Kotlin
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/json-to-kotlin

Convert JSON to Kotlin data classes.

### JSON to Python
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/json-to-python

Convert JSON to Python dataclasses or Pydantic BaseModel classes. Features:
- Two output styles: Python dataclasses and Pydantic models
- Auto-converts camelCase keys to snake_case
- Pydantic mode adds Field(alias=...) when keys differ from snake_case
- Multiple JSON sample support for detecting Optional fields
- Usage example generation

Useful for:
- Building Python APIs from JSON response schemas
- Generating Pydantic models for FastAPI
- Creating dataclasses for data processing pipelines

### JSON to Go
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/json-to-go

Convert JSON to Go structs with proper json tags.

### JSON to Rust
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/json-to-rust

Convert JSON to Rust structs with serde derive macros.

### JSON to JSDoc
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/json-to-jsdoc

Convert JSON to JSDoc type definitions for JavaScript documentation.

---

## Generators

### UUID Generator
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/uuid-generator

Generate UUID v4 (random) identifiers. Uses `crypto.randomUUID()` or `crypto.getRandomValues()` for cryptographic randomness.

### QR Code Generator
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/qr-code-generator

Generate QR codes from text, URLs, or any data. Uses Canvas API for rendering.

### Lorem Ipsum Generator
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/lorem-ipsum-generator

Generate placeholder text for designs. Options for paragraphs, sentences, or words.

### Random Number Generator
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/random-number-generator

Generate random numbers with configurable range. Includes code samples in multiple programming languages.

---

## AI-Powered Tools

All AI tools run models directly in the browser using Transformers.js (WebAssembly/WebGPU). No data is sent to external AI services.

### JS Tokenizers
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/js-tokenizer

Tokenize text using various AI model tokenizers. Useful for:
- Estimating token counts for API pricing
- Understanding how models see your text
- Debugging prompt engineering

### RAG Chunker
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/rag-chunker

Split text into retrieval-friendly chunks with configurable overlap and token counts. Essential for building RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) applications.

### Sentiment Analysis
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/sentiment-analysis

Analyze the emotional tone of text (positive, negative, neutral) using in-browser AI models.

### Zero-Shot Classification
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/zero-shot-classification

Classify text into custom categories without training. Define your own labels and classify text instantly.

### Sentence Similarity
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/sentence-similarity

Generate text embeddings and calculate semantic similarity between sentences or documents.

### Question Answering
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/question-answering

Extract answers from a given context passage. Provide context and ask questions about it.

### Fill-Mask Text Completion
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/fill-mask

Predict missing words in sentences using masked language models.

### Text Summarization
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/text-summarization

Summarize long text into concise overviews using AI.

### Text Extractor
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/text-extractor

Extract structured data from unstructured text:
- Email addresses
- URLs
- IP addresses
- Dates
- Phone numbers
- Custom patterns (regex)

---

## Utility Tools

### Color Picker
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/color-picker

Pick colors and convert between formats:
- HEX (#RRGGBB)
- RGB (red, green, blue)
- HSL (hue, saturation, lightness)
- HSV (hue, saturation, value)
- CMYK (cyan, magenta, yellow, black)

### API Tester
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/api-tester

Test REST API endpoints and view responses. Supports:
- GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH methods
- Custom headers
- Request body
- Response inspection

Note: This is the only tool that makes external HTTP requests, and only to URLs explicitly provided by the user.

### HTTP Status Code Reference
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/http-status-codes

Searchable reference of all HTTP status codes with descriptions and use cases. Covers 1xx through 5xx ranges. Useful for:
- Looking up unfamiliar status codes
- Understanding proper status code usage for APIs
- Debugging HTTP response codes

### iOS App Lookup
**URL**: https://developers.do/tools/ios-app-lookup

Look up iOS app metadata from the App Store using bundle ID. Returns app name, description, version, ratings, and more.

---

## iOS Apps

### Terremoto
**URL**: https://terremoto.developers.do

iOS app for tracking earthquakes in real-time. Features:
- Real-time earthquake alerts
- Interactive map with earthquake locations
- Historical earthquake data
- Customizable notification settings

### FedPulse
**URL**: https://fedpulse.developers.do

iOS app to search, save, and track federal government jobs. Features:
- Search USAJobs listings
- Save and track favorite positions
- Job alerts and notifications
- Filter by agency, location, and salary

### FAA Part 107 Quiz Prep
**URL**: https://part107.developers.do

iOS app for FAA Part 107 drone pilot certification exam preparation. Features:
- Practice questions for the FAA Part 107 exam
- Study mode and test simulation
- Track your progress
- Comprehensive coverage of drone regulations

### Citizenship Test Prep
**URL**: https://citizenshiptest.developers.do

iOS app for US citizenship naturalization test preparation. Features:
- All 100 civics test questions
- Practice tests and flashcards
- Audio pronunciation of questions and answers
- Track your study progress

### ClawPet
**URL**: https://clawpet.clawpantry.com

iOS virtual desktop pet that visualizes bot activity in real-time. Features:
- Real-time state sync via WebSocket with sub-second latency
- Dual transport with WebSocket and HTTP polling automatic failover
- Five distinct mood states with unique animations
- Private by default with no account or cloud required

---

## Privacy Commitment

DevTools is built on a strict privacy-first philosophy:

1. **Anonymized Analytics**: We use Google Analytics with consent controls. Analytics default to anonymized mode (no cookies, no personal data). Users can accept full analytics, keep anonymized, or reject all tracking.
2. **No Tracking Cookies by Default**: Tracking cookies are only set if the user explicitly accepts full analytics
3. **No Server Processing**: All tool operations happen in your browser
4. **No Data Retention**: We don't store any user input or output
5. **No Account Required**: Use all tools without signing up
6. **Open Source**: Verify our claims by reviewing the code

---

## Technical Stack

- **Framework**: Next.js 16.1.1 (Static Export)
- **UI**: React 19.2.3, Tailwind CSS 4.1.18
- **Language**: TypeScript 5.9.3
- **AI Runtime**: Transformers.js (WASM/WebGPU)
- **Crypto**: Web Crypto API
- **Package Manager**: Bun

---

## Contact

Website: https://developers.do
